trending_topics.context: 2 posts, 1.65x baseline rate — mentioning Uniswap, Ethereum, Vitalik

sample_posts.text:
Vitalik Unveils Lean Ethereum Upgrade Roadmap
> "Lean Ethereum" is a collection of improvements that will come online to the Ethereum network over the course of three or four years.
> To upgrade core infra with recursive STARKs, quantum-safe cryptography, privacy, and scalable new state models while minimizing disruption to existing apps, with the vision to make Ethereum simpler, faster, more secure, and scalable.
Source:
https://x.com/VitalikButerin/status/2073459000398463446

tweet.text:
Two weeks ago, Ethereum researchers met in Berlin to continue charting the protocol's long-term trajectory, following along discussions with client teams in Svalbard in April.

The updated strawmap is at http://strawmap.org, and I attached a picture of it to this post.

My own high-level takeaways:

* "Lean Ethereum" is not a single one-shot upgrade, it is a collection of improvements that will come online to the Ethereum network over the course of three or four years. But make no mistake, this IS the third major iteration of Ethereum in the same way that the Merge was the second. Almost every

sample_posts.text:
"Lean Ethereum" roadmap
Ethereum’s multi-year roadmap of major upgrades that will simplify and fundamentally upgrade core parts of the protocol to make it much more scalable, secure, and quantum-resistant
https://x.com/vitalikbuterin/status/2073459000398463446?s=12&t=2q9OLMlaobNlHLewOXT4uA

sample_posts.text:
Morgan Stanley projects humanoid robots as the largest embodied AI opportunity
Targeting a $7.5 trillion TAM by 2050 with a global stock of 1 billion units
https://x.com/pequityresearch/status/2073479817245147424?s=12&t=2q9OLMlaobNlHLewOXT4uA

tweet.text:
Morgan Stanley: Robotics

> Humanoid robots represent the largest embodied AI opportunity, with a projected global total addressable market (TAM) of US$7.5 trillion by 2050, accumulating an estimated global stock of 1 billion robots.

> While the industry is in its infancy, Morgan Stanley forecasts that small-scale commercialization and pilot deployments will begin this year (2026).

> The global humanoid component market is projected to reach a TAM of US$780 billion by 2040. Key hardware components are expected to see massive demand multipliers by 2050 compared to 2025 levels, led by Edge Com

tweet.text:
Markets were jittery this week on news reports that Meta may be selling compute, raising concerns about excess supply. We thought we’d share some perspective using our rental term curves on why this news, if true, doesn’t have to be bearish for GPU rental prices.

Last year, ahead of the agentic AI boom, Meta aggressively locked in a large amount of compute capacity. It quite possibly secured more than it ultimately needed. It was a smart strategic bet to secure scarce supply early, with the flexibility to either deploy it internally or sell excess into a tight market. They acquired valuable r

tweet.text:
Markets were jittery this week on news reports that Meta may be selling compute, raising concerns about excess supply. We thought we’d share some perspective using our rental term curves on why this news, if true, doesn’t have to be bearish for GPU rental prices.

Last year, ahead of the agentic AI boom, Meta aggressively locked in a large amount of compute capacity. It quite possibly secured more than it ultimately needed. It was a smart strategic bet to secure scarce supply early, with the flexibility to either deploy it internally or sell excess into a tight market. They acquired valuable r

sample_posts.text:
Morgan Stanley projects humanoid robots as the largest embodied AI opportunity
Targeting a $7.5 trillion TAM by 2050 with a global stock of 1 billion units
https://x.com/pequityresearch/status/2073479817245147424?s=12&t=2q9OLMlaobNlHLewOXT4uA

tweet.text:
Morgan Stanley: Robotics

> Humanoid robots represent the largest embodied AI opportunity, with a projected global total addressable market (TAM) of US$7.5 trillion by 2050, accumulating an estimated global stock of 1 billion robots.

> While the industry is in its infancy, Morgan Stanley forecasts that small-scale commercialization and pilot deployments will begin this year (2026).

> The global humanoid component market is projected to reach a TAM of US$780 billion by 2040. Key hardware components are expected to see massive demand multipliers by 2050 compared to 2025 levels, led by Edge Com

convergence.context: 3 posts across 3 channels (cookiesreads, shoalresearch, ahboyashreads) — mentioning Morgan Stanley, Vitalik, H100, Curve

sample_posts.text:
Vitalik Unveils Lean Ethereum Upgrade Roadmap
> "Lean Ethereum" is a collection of improvements that will come online to the Ethereum network over the course of three or four years.
> To upgrade core infra with recursive STARKs, quantum-safe cryptography, privacy, and scalable new state models while minimizing disruption to existing apps, with the vision to make Ethereum simpler, faster, more secure, and scalable.
Source:
https://x.com/VitalikButerin/status/2073459000398463446

tweet.text:
Two weeks ago, Ethereum researchers met in Berlin to continue charting the protocol's long-term trajectory, following along discussions with client teams in Svalbard in April.

The updated strawmap is at http://strawmap.org, and I attached a picture of it to this post.

My own high-level takeaways:

* "Lean Ethereum" is not a single one-shot upgrade, it is a collection of improvements that will come online to the Ethereum network over the course of three or four years. But make no mistake, this IS the third major iteration of Ethereum in the same way that the Merge was the second. Almost every
